steps.title: Simplify the left side and isolate the square root
steps.content:
Start with $$\frac{12x}{6y}=2\sqrt{w+22}$$
Simplify the left side: $$\frac{12x}{6y}=\frac{2x}{y}$$
Divide both sides by 2 to isolate the square root: $$\frac{x}{y}=\sqrt{w+22}$$

steps.title: Square both sides and solve for w
steps.content:
Square both sides to eliminate the square root: $$\left(\frac{x}{y}\right)^2=w+22$$
Subtract 22 from both sides to isolate $w$: $$w=\left(\frac{x}{y}\right)^2-22$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.
